title: Patient symptom intake chatbot (frontend UI, guardrails, and backend wiring)

requirements.summary: Build a chatbot-style screen that lets users enter free-text symptoms and view a chronological transcript of user/assistant messages with validation for empty submissions.
acceptanceCriteria: A chat screen is available with an input box, send button, and scrollable transcript.
Submitting symptoms adds the user message to the transcript and shows a generated assistant reply.
The UI handles empty input (cannot submit) and shows a friendly validation message.

file_operations.description: Create the app shell that renders the chat screen as the primary view (single-screen app) and provides overall layout structure for transcript + composer.

Implement the chat UI: scrollable transcript, message bubbles with clear user vs assistant styling, input box + send button, and empty-input validation messaging. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Input/Textarea, Card/ScrollArea) to compose the UI. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Define lightweight frontend types for chat transcript items (role, text, timestamp, status) to keep UI state predictable and chronological.

requirements.summary: Add visible medical safety guardrails to assistant replies (disclaimer always; red-flag escalation; avoid patient-specific dosing unless provided).
acceptanceCriteria: Every assistant response includes an English disclaimer that the app is not a doctor and is for informational purposes.

If the input mentions red flags (e.g., chest pain, difficulty breathing, severe allergic reaction, signs of stroke, severe dehydration, blood in stool/vomit, suicidal ideation), the response prioritizes urgent-care guidance and does not present casual OTC-only advice.
Responses avoid exact pediatric dosing or weight-based dosing unless the user provided age/weight; otherwise the response recommends consulting a clinician/pharmacist for dosing.

file_operations.description: Add deterministic client-side post-processing for chatbot replies: (1) append/ensure a visible English disclaimer in every assistant message, (2) detect red-flag phrases in the user's input and elevate urgent-care guidance, (3) add a dosing caution when age/weight is not present. Keep logic conservative and rule-based.

Wire guardrail post-processing into rendering of assistant messages and add a persistent, visible disclaimer element near the transcript (e.g., a banner/alert). Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Alert) for the disclaimer presentation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Connect chat sending to the existing backend actor via React Query with loading, error, and retry UX.
acceptanceCriteria: When a message is sent, the UI shows a loading indicator until the backend returns.
If the backend call fails, the UI shows a non-technical error message and allows retry.
Successful replies render in the transcript with clear visual distinction between user and assistant messages.

file_operations.description: Implement a React Query mutation that calls the backend actor's getTreatment method using the existing useActor pattern, returning the assistant reply string and surfacing loading/error state.

On send: add the user message immediately, trigger the mutation, render an in-transcript loading state for the assistant, and on success render the assistant reply (after applying guardrails). On failure, render a friendly error message with a retry action. Use shadcn-ui components (e.g., Button, Alert, Skeleton/Loader patterns if available) for states. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a small mapper that builds the Symptom argument expected by backend.d.ts (including creator principal selection and description) so ChatPage stays UI-focused.

requirements.summary: Add a simple onboarding/intake prompt that encourages key context (age group, pregnancy status, duration, severity, allergies, meds, chronic conditions) while allowing free-form use.
acceptanceCriteria: The chat screen includes a short, English prompt (inline or first assistant message) listing suggested details to include.
The user can proceed without filling structured fields, but the UI clearly suggests what information improves accuracy.

file_operations.description: Define the onboarding/intake prompt copy (English) listing the suggested details to include (age group, pregnancy status, duration, severity, allergies, current meds, chronic conditions).
Show the intake prompt as the first assistant message in the transcript (or a prominent inline hint above the composer) without forcing structured fields.

requirements.summary: Apply a consistent, accessible healthcare-appropriate visual theme across the chat experience without using a blue/purple primary palette.
acceptanceCriteria: The app has a consistent theme (background, surfaces, spacing, and typography) applied to the chat view and any header/onboarding elements.
The chosen palette does not rely on blue/purple as the primary colors.
The UI is readable and accessible (sufficient contrast for text, clear focus states for inputs/buttons).

file_operations.description: Create global styles and theme tokens (CSS variables used by Tailwind/shadcn-ui) with a non-blue/non-purple primary palette (e.g., green/teal + warm neutrals), and ensure clear focus styles and readable defaults.

Align chat layout, spacing, typography, and surfaces to the global theme for a healthcare assistant look-and-feel, ensuring adequate contrast and keyboard-focus visibility. Use shadcn-ui components to maintain consistent styling. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Adjust Tailwind theme extensions only as needed to support the chosen non-blue/non-purple palette and consistent typography/spacing utilities across the chat UI.
